title: Crispy Umami Kenyan Chicken Biryani
description: A fragrant, protein-packed rice dish with crispy chicken and umami-rich sauces, perfect as a hangover cure and a comforting KenyanIndian fusion.
instructions: Rinse rice in cold water until runoff is clear, then soak in a bowl for 30 minutes.
In a shallow dish, combine chicken pieces with 1 tbsp soy sauce, ½ tsp fish sauce, ginger paste, salt, and pepper; let marinate for 10 minutes.

Heat 1 tbsp ghee in a large skillet over medium‑high heat. Add cumin seeds and wait until they splutter. Add sliced onion and sauté until caramelized, about 7 minutes. Add garlic and cook for an additional 30 seconds.

Add tomato paste to the onions, stir for 1 minute until fragrant. Add the marinated chicken, searing each side until golden. Pour in remaining soy sauce and fish sauce; cook for 5 minutes to let flavors meld.

Drain the soaked rice and add it to the skillet, stirring to coat the grains with the onion‑chicken mixture. Pour in 4 cups of water, add a pinch of salt, bring to a boil, then reduce heat to a simmer, cover, and cook for 15 minutes or until the rice is tender.
Meanwhile, in a separate pot, bring 2 cups water to a rolling boil. Add the soaked rice, sprinkle garam masala, and simmer gently for 10 minutes, then drain.

Fold the cooked rice into the skillet with chicken, drizzle with a splash of hot water if needed, and sprinkle the remaining coriander powder over the top. Cover loosely and let steam for 5 minutes to meld the flavors.
Finish the dish by adding a small knob of ghee for sheen, and garnish with fresh cilantro if desired. Serve immediately hot.
